eft: noun. A newt, especially a smooth newt (Lissotriton vulgaris, syn.
feint: noun. A movement made to confuse an opponent; a dummy.
fen: noun. A type of wetland fed by ground water and runoff, containing peat below the waterline, characteristically alkaline.
fie: interjection. Sometimes followed by on or upon: used to express distaste, disgust, or outrage.
fin: noun. One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre / maneuver.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
finite: adjective. Having an end or limit; constrained by bounds; whose number of elements is a natural number.
infinite: adjective. Indefinably large, countlessly great; immense.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
intine: noun. The innermost wall of a spore or pollen grain
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tine: noun. A spike or point on an implement or tool, especially a prong of a fork or a tooth of a comb.
feni: noun. An alcoholic drink made in Goa, India, from fermented cashew apples or coconut toddy (sap).
iii: numeral. Ⅲ, the Roman numeral three (3).
infit: noun. An inlier - sensitive or information - weighted fit
nef: noun. An extravagant table ornament and container used in the Middle Ages and Renaissance, made in the shape of a ship.
neti: noun. Nasal irrigation performed using a neti pot as part of Hatha yoga.
nief: noun. A serf or bondsman born into servitude.
nife: noun. The innermost layers or core of the Earth, composed chiefly of nickel and iron.
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
tef: noun. Used as a specific epithet to indicate a kind of grass
